sources.title: National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine, Convergence: Facilitating Transdisciplinary Integration of Life Sciences, Physical Sciences, Engineering, and Beyond (2014).
sources.note: A foundational account of how complex problems increasingly require integration across fields.

sources.title: OECD, Mission-Oriented Innovation.
sources.note: Explains how public institutions can convene actors around complex problems that do not yield to isolated interventions.

sources.title: Stanford Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence, 2025 AI Index Report: Policy and Governance.
sources.note: Tracks the growth of AI-related policy and regulatory activity.

sources.title: OECD, AI Principles, adopted in 2019 and updated in 2024.
sources.note: Sets out an international framework for trustworthy and human-centred AI.

sources.title: National Institute of Standards and Technology, Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework.
sources.note: Provides practical guidance for identifying and managing AI risks.

sources.title: European Commission, AI Act: The First-Ever Legal Framework on AI.
sources.note: Describes the European Union's risk-based legal framework for AI.

sources.title: National Institute of Standards and Technology, CHIPS for America.
sources.note: Connects semiconductor manufacturing with research, workforce, supply-chain resilience, and national competitiveness.

sources.title: The White House, National Strategy for the Arctic Region (2022).
sources.note: Links security, climate, economic development, Indigenous livelihoods, and international cooperation in the Arctic.

sources.title: NATO, Defence Innovation Accelerator for the North Atlantic (DIANA).
sources.note: Connects emerging technology with collective defence and security challenges.
